title: [InstCombine] Combine and->cmp->sel->or-disjoint into and->mul
summary: The developers generalized a pattern that converts OR-of-selects into a single AND-MUL sequence. The key insight was recognizing that `(!(A & N) ? 0 : N*C) + (!(A & M) ? 0 : M*C)` simplifies to `A & (N+M) * C` when masks are single-bit powers-of-two and the per-bit constants are proportional. They extracted the core logic using `decomposeBitTest()` to unify various forms: different predicates (eq/ne), vector types, and truncations. The transformation validates that masks are distinct powers-of-two, zero/non-zero select arms match the predicate, and the scaled constant `C` is identical across both selects. This approach cleanly handles edge cases like overlapping masks, multi-bit masks (rejected), and vector splats/non-splats. The lesson: identify the underlying mathematical relationship, factor out predicate and mask extraction, then enforce constraints that guarantee correctness across variations.
